title: Implement Listing And Show Commands
description: Add `spec list` and `spec show` with metadata rendering across all folders, filter support, legacy-spec behavior, and bounded body preview output for `show`.
acceptance_criteria: `spec list` prints specs from all three lifecycle folders and supports `--status`, `--tag`, and `--priority` filters.
Legacy specs appear in `list` with `—` for missing metadata and inferred names.
`spec show` resolves by ID and partial filename, prints YAML frontmatter when present, and includes the first section preview bounded by separator or 20 lines.
`spec show` on a legacy spec prints the required warning line and raw content.

handoff_summary.completed: - `cmd_list`: verified complete — scans all three lifecycle folders, shows legacy specs with `—` placeholders and inferred names, supports `--status`, `--tag`, `--priority` filters.\n- `cmd_show`: fixed legacy-spec path to print warning line + full raw content (no truncation). Regular specs still get frontmatter YAML block + first-section preview bounded by `---` separator or 20 lines.\n- Syntax check passes.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester should verify: `spec list` across all folders with and without filters, `spec show` on a spec with frontmatter (preview truncation), `spec show` on a legacy spec (full raw content, warning line).

execution_history.summary: Deferred due to file-scope conflict with active bead B-9ba25868

Implemented `spec list` and `spec show` commands in `skills/spec-management/spec.py`. `cmd_list` was already fully implemented by the dependency bead (B-31034e6e) with all three lifecycle folders, legacy-spec `—` placeholders, and `--status`/`--tag`/`--priority` filter support. `cmd_show` had a structural bug: the legacy-spec branch fell through to the body-printing loop, truncating legacy content at 20 lines or the first `---` separator — violating AC4 which requires full raw content. Fixed by restructuring `cmd_show` to print `warning: <path> is a legacy spec — no frontmatter` then write the full raw body via `sys.stdout.write(body)` and return early, leaving the 20-line bounded preview path only for specs with valid frontmatter.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `uv run python -c "import codex_orchestrator"` or `uv run python -m py_compile <file>`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not `unittest discover`, not `unittest tests.<module>`, not any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
